vision_report.biome_observations: The soil in the pots (T-5 to T-1) appeared consistently dark and devoid of vegetative growth. No fungal or incidental growth was detected. The final image (CURRENT) represents a complete sensor or environmental obstruction.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: From T-5 to T-1, the biome remained stagnant with no growth or change. At CURRENT, the visual field transitioned from a top-down view of the desk to a featureless, grey-toned obstruction.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is non-viable. The absence of any green tissue across the entire 5-day sequence confirms that the plants were either already deceased or removed prior to the start of the observation period.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the total absence of the registered plants throughout the entire sequence, followed by the complete loss of visual input in the final frame.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of botanical life. The pots were empty of foliage throughout the observation window. The sequence concludes with a total loss of visual data, suggesting either a camera malfunction, a lens obstruction, or the removal of the entire experimental setup from the desk.
